findings.title: US 5-year breakeven inflation rate hits highest since Feb 2025
findings.summary: Bond investor expectations for average inflation over next 5 years rose 26bps since Middle East conflict began, reaching highest level since Feb 2025. This signals heightened inflation risks amid energy shocks, pressuring Fed rate cut odds. Impacts bond yields and real returns across portfolios.
findings.claim_excerpt: five-year breakeven rate... has risen 26 basis points since the conflict began—hitting the highest level since February 2025
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term inflation repricing tied to energy shock duration, likely weeks/months to resolve.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Upside inflation risks embed higher term premia in bonds, delaying easing cycles globally.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Direct market data from authoritative weekly economic update with Fed context.

findings.title: Fed members divide on 2026 rate cuts: 12/19 expect at least one
findings.summary: Fed policy committee voted 11-1 to hold rates, but forecasts show split with 12 of 19 members predicting cuts while 7 expect none. Powell emphasizes uncertainty from energy prices. Futures imply 60% chance of no change through 2026, biasing tight policy.
findings.claim_excerpt: 12 of the 19 members predicted at least one interest rate cut this year while seven said there will be no cut
findings.signal_type_basis: Conditional on energy shock evolution, with medium-term policy implications.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Divided dot plot reinforces higher-for-longer regime amid inflation upside.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from Confirmed: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Detailed FOMC vote and forecast breakdown from reliable economic monitor.

findings.review_reason: Track dot plot evolution for cut probability shifts.

findings.title: ECB holds rates citing Middle East conflict inflation risks
findings.summary: ECB left benchmark rates unchanged, noting Middle East conflict creates upside inflation risks via energy and downside growth risks. Material near-term inflation impact expected. Investor rate expectations revised higher for ECB and BOE.
findings.claim_excerpt: conflict “in the Middle East has made the outlook significantly more uncertain, creating upside risks for inflation
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term dependent on conflict intensity and energy pass-through.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Global policy convergence on caution elevates Eurozone bond yields.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from High: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Timely ECB decision summary with direct quotes.

findings.title: US effective tariff rate at 11.4% as of October 2025, highest since 1943
findings.summary: Average effective US tariff rate reached 11.4% in October 2025, highest since 1943 amid Liberation Day package. Reflects T3 escalation with retaliation. Sustained high rates embed protectionist regime in trade flows.
findings.claim_excerpt: average effective tariff rate for the U.S. stood at 11.4 percent... highest rate since 1943
findings.signal_type_basis: Regime-level trade policy shift playing out over years.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Elevated tariffs pressure EM growth and metal supply chains long-term.
findings.confidence_basis: Official Fed data with historical context.

Fed... higher energy prices will push up overall inflation

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Richmond Fed tier-1 data corroborated by tariff regime context.

findings.title: Powell: Energy shock effects unknown, Fed adopts wait-and-see
findings.summary: Fed Chair Powell states higher energy prices from conflict will push inflation but scope/duration unknown. Policy committee takes wait-and-see approach. Raises uncertainty premium across assets.
findings.claim_excerpt: higher energy prices will push up overall inflation, but it is too soon to know the scope and duration
findings.signal_type_basis: Episodic tied to conflict; noise unless sustained 3+ weeks.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Delays rate cuts, supports energy as tactical hedge.

findings.review_reason: Check energy price sustainment for TS to CC upgrade.

findings.title: Market prices 60% odds of Fed rates unchanged through 2026
findings.summary: Futures market implies 60% probability Fed holds benchmark rate steady for rest of 2026 post-FOMC. Reflects inflation forecast upgrades. Anchors short-end bond pricing higher.
findings.claim_excerpt: futures market is now showing a 60% implied probability that the Fed will leave the benchmark rate unchanged
findings.preliminary_assessment: Reduces easing tailwinds for risk assets.

findings.title: Fed and ECB highlight energy-driven inflation uncertainty
findings.summary: Both Fed and ECB cite Middle East conflict for higher energy prices pushing near-term inflation with uncertain medium-term effects. Creates synchronized downside growth/upside CPI risks. Pressures global bonds and commodities.
findings.claim_excerpt: higher energy prices will push up overall inflation... conflict has made the outlook significantly more uncertain
findings.signal_type_basis: Cross-central bank consensus on energy shock duration.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Reinforces stagflationary tilt, favoring energy over growth assets.
findings.confidence_basis: [AUTO-DOWNGRADED from Confirmed: single source at Tier 0 — Analyst cron to verify] Multiple CB statements in one report.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Dual CB coverage validates cross-Atlantic alignment.

findings.review_reason: Monitor CB divergence on energy pass-through.
